segments.title: Planning a focused research path for D-class cruisers
segments.summary: Dr. Clarke uses a D-class cruiser project to show how a precise question, book indexes, and a balanced key text can turn a large naval-history library into a manageable research path.
segments.body: This practical example applies academic planning directly to naval-history research on the D-class cruisers. Once the class is defined as the subject, Dr. Clarke checks book indexes and recognizable ship names instead of reading every apparently relevant volume from cover to cover. The method quickly reduces one large book to a single useful page and helps him reject sources that do not address the class. He then chooses a balanced encyclopedia entry of several pages as the key text, preferring a foundation that can guide further reading without overwhelming the project with technical detail. Opening the video here shows how a research plan can control source selection before the writing begins.

evidence.note: Dr. Clarke demonstrates narrowing a D-class cruiser project through indexes, recognizable ship names, relevant pages, and a balanced key text.
